mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in unemployment rate below 4% signals tight labor markets that boost corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in low UNRATE regimes are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust economic demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through improved corporate revenue outlooks.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals from GDPC1 and HOUST fail to produce SPX 60d returns > 0 in > 60% of cases or Rank IC < 0.02 over 5-year horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2% annually triggers central bank rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns remain positive in > 60% of instances with CPIAUCSL > 2% YoY or Rank IC > 0.04 over 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 95 signals weak manufacturing activity, reducing corporate earnings expectations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain negative in < 40% of INDPRO contraction instances or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 150k concurrent with rising wage pressures (CPIAUCSL) indicates inflationary labor market dynamics, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signals of PAYEMS > 150k and CPIAUCSL > 2% fail to produce SPX 5d negative returns in > 60% of cases or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit (RSAFS) above $400B indicates heightened household leverage, increasing vulnerability to rate hikes and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns remain negative in < 40% of RSAFS > 400B instances or Rank IC < 0.02 over 5-year horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and rising oil prices (CPIAUCSL) signal supply-side shocks that increase inflation and reduce corporate margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signals of INDPRO < 97.5 and CPIAUCSL > 2% fail to produce SPX 20d negative returns in > 60% of cases or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year window.
